items.headline: Oil and Gas Jump After US Seizure of Iranian Ship Imperils Talks
items.summary: Oil and natural gas prices soared after the US Navy seized an Iranian ship during a chaotic weekend that saw Tehran firing at vessels and reimposing controls in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: How Niche Insurance Shielded Bad Bunny From Bad Weather
items.summary: Bad Bunny had a problem. Days before the Puerto Rican mega-star was set to perform in Colombia earlier this year, the possibility of heavy rains threatened to turn three sold-out shows in Medellin into multimillion-dollar losses.

items.headline: Gold Falls as Renewed Hormuz Disruption Stokes Inflation Concern
items.summary: Gold fell after a weekend flare-up in Middle Eastern waters renewed inflation risks from an energy-supply shock and cast doubt over talks to end the war.

items.headline: Has the U.S. Seizure of an Iranian Ship Doomed Peace Talks? | Insight with Haslinda Amin 04/20/2026
items.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Hormuz Standoff Keeps Markets on Edge | The Asia Trade 4/20/2026
items.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Avril Hong, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: A Stock Trader’s Guide to a Fractured Economic World
items.summary: Investors are set to pour more money into defense, energy and technology stocks as the Middle East war forces governments to prioritize security and become more self reliant.

items.headline: Ninety One Sees South Africa Opportunities in War Volatility
items.summary: Ninety One Plc remains constructive on South African equities, saying the war in Iran has created opportunities to buy stocks at prices that are lower than justified by their earnings outlook.

items.headline: Revolut CEO Storonsky Says Digital Bank’s IPO Is Two Years Out
items.summary: Revolut Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Nik Storonsky said he wants to take the London-based digital bank public, but not until at least 2028, extending the timeline on one of Europe’s most anticipated offerings.

items.headline: Oil Jumps on Iran Tensions, US Stock Futures Drop: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Oil jumped and US equity-index futures retreated as traders turned cautious following a weekend flare-up in US-Iran tensions, curbing optimism that strains in the Middle East were easing.

items.headline: Stocks Set to Fall on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
Copper Wavers Near Two-Month High as US-Iran Tensions Escalate
items.summary: Copper eased from its highest close since early February, as peace talks between Washington and Tehran were thrown into uncertainty after the US seized an Iranian ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Investors Bet ‘Peak Uncertainty’ Has Passed as Asia Stocks Climb
items.summary: Asian stocks rose Monday as investors looked past renewed Middle East tensions over the weekend, shifting focus back to fundamentals and the prospect of further talks.

items.headline: Zimbabwe’s Currency Is Undervalued by Nearly Half, Governor Says
items.summary: Zimbabwe’s currency is undervalued by almost half, the governor of the central bank said, pointing to the foreign reserves and gold that back the unit.

items.headline: Germany’s Sovereign Wealth Fund Is Dropping Weapons Exclusions
items.summary: Germany’s sovereign wealth fund is abandoning long-held restrictions on investing in weapons manufacturers, as it responds to an increasingly tense geopolitical reality.

items.headline: Iran War Deepens China’s Dependence on the US for Niche Gas
items.summary: China is set to import a record volume of US ethane this month as petrochemical producers seek alternative feedstocks for their operations after the war in the Middle East choked off crucial supplies.

items.headline: US Seizes Iranian Ship, Peace Talks in Doubt
items.summary:
Over the weekend, the US Navy fired upon and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman. It's the first seizure in the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

President Donald Trump and Iranian officials offered disparate views on the next stage of the war, casting uncertainty over whether the two sides would meet for peace talks with a ceasefire set to expire in the coming days. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Iron Ore Gains With China Demand Resilient Ahead of May Day
items.summary: Iron ore rose as Chinese demand remained strong ahead of the May Day holidays while near-term supply was constrained.

items.headline: European Natural Gas Rises as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again
items.summary: European natural gas rose after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz shortly after reopening the waterway, choking the flow of energy from the Persian Gulf.

items.headline: US-Iran Peace Talks in Doubt After Weekend Chaos | The China Show 4/20/2026
items.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, David Ingles and Yvonne Man give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Beijing
items.summary: Bloomberg's Minmin Low highlights a half marathon held in Beijing, where autonomous robots showcased significant technological advancements including beating a human record. The event showcases China's rapid advances in robotics and artificial intelligence. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: AirAsia Tests Market With Deutsche-Led $230 Million Private Debt
items.summary: Deutsche Bank AG is marketing a $230 million private-credit deal for Malaysian budget airline AirAsia Aviation Group, according to people familiar with the matter, testing investor demand for the carrier amid rising fuel prices.

items.headline: JPMorgan Sees Year‑Long Path Back to $75 Brent
items.summary: Parsley Ong, Asia Energy & Chemicals Research Head at JPMorgan, explains the scenarios her team has mapped out for oil prices as tensions continue to simmer in the Strait of Hormuz. She speaks on Bloomberg's The China Show. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Taiwan Weighs US Dollar Dividend Payouts for Listed Companies
items.summary: Taiwan is weighing a proposal to permit listed companies to distribute dividends in US dollars, a change from the rule requiring payouts in the local currency, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.headline: Asia Hedge Funds Log Losses From Iran War Before Ceasefire
items.summary: To see how Asian hedge funds got blindsided by the Iran war, look no further than Trivest Advisors Ltd.

items.headline: Markets Get in Tune to the Strait and Narrow
items.summary: Earnings hopes counter the volatile lack of message discipline by the US and Iran.

items.headline: AI’s Token Economy Revolution Creates New China Tech Winners
items.summary: China’s cheap artificial intelligence models are rapidly attracting global users and creating new winners in the nation’s stock market.

items.headline: Singapore Is Procuring More LNG as Iran War Cuts Some Supply
items.summary: Singapore is getting additional liquefied natural gas from outside of the Middle East, as the war in Iran chokes supply from the region, according to a government body.

items.headline: Emerging-Market Stocks Erase War Losses on AI Demand, Peace Hope
items.summary: Emerging-market shares recouped their Iran war-induced losses, lifted by a rebound in Asian equities driven by optimism about artificial intelligence and lingering hopes for fresh Middle East tensions to ease.

items.headline: Credit Growth to Lift India Bank Profits Despite Likely FX Losses
items.summary: Strong credit growth helped keep fourth-quarter numbers healthy at Indian banks, numbers from HDFC Bank Ltd. and ICICI Bank Ltd. showed on Saturday, though the latter reported a treasury loss linked to the central bank’s rupee derivatives curbs.

items.headline: China to Begin Special Bond Sales With Record 30-Year Offering
items.summary: China will kick off this year’s ultra-long special government bond sales with a record offering of 30-year notes on Friday, in a move that will offer a gauge of investor demand.

events.event_name: U.S. dollar seen strengthening as U.S.-Israel strikes intensify
OPEC+ agrees modest oil output boost even as US war on Iran disrupts shipments
